query: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool repair status latest news June July 2026, algae paint peeling, is the pool fully repaired, lawsuit status
answer:
Research complete. Here is a summary of my findings on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool repair status:

## Key Findings (as of July 14, 2026)

**The pool is NOT fully repaired.** It has been drained a second time (starting July 10, 2026) for additional repairs. As of today it holds only algae and debris, with no clear timeline for refilling.

**Algae:** Bloomed within a day of the June refilling. DOI called it "residual." Remediation used hydrogen peroxide and nanobubble ozone technology — but the nanobubble treatment "may have led to the new blue finish peeling off."

**Paint peeling:** The "American flag blue" polyurea coating peeled in multiple sections within ~two weeks. Trump blamed vandalism (alleging a "350-foot gash"), but "no evidence of the gash had emerged" when the pool was drained.

**Vandalism:** At least 4 people charged; ex-Olympian David Hearn indicted, pleaded not guilty.

**Costs:** Ballooned from an initial ~$1–2M claim to a $14.7M no-bid contract, ~$16M with cleanup. Trump projected "less than $20 million."

**Lawsuit:** The Cultural Landscape Foundation sued NPS/DOI/Burgum on May 11, 2026 under the National Historic Preservation Act. Judge Carl J. Nichols never granted an injunction, and the project finished June 3, 2026 before the court ruled. The case remains active/ongoing but failed to halt the resurfacing.

The full report, structured data points, and 10 sources have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool.

sources.title: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool drained for repairs again, Trump says
sources.snippet: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool has again been drained in Washington, D.C., on Tuesday as Trump claims the damage done by 'vandals' is being repaired. More than $14 million has been spent.

sources.title: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool drained again after algae, paint issues
sources.snippet: Pool drained again July 10, 2026 to address algae, paint peeling, and July 4 debris; ~$14M ballooned cost; Trump alleged 350-foot gash by vandals.

sources.title: Was the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool drained? Here's the latest
sources.snippet: Crews began draining the 6.5-million-gallon basin Friday July 10; Atlantic Industrial Coatings handling repairs again; no evidence of the alleged 350-foot gash emerged.

sources.title: Timeline: Trump's 2026 Reflecting Pool renovation project
sources.snippet: Full timeline: April announcement, $14M+ spending, algae bloom, nanobubble tech linked to peeling, $1.7M cleanup, ~$16M total, May 2026 lawsuit over blue paint.

sources.title: Some of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool liner appears to be falling apart
sources.snippet: Blue coating peeling in sections; over $15M spent after three months; hydrogen peroxide and nanobubble ozone treatments; no-bid contracts citing urgency.

sources.title: 'Residual algae' coats part of newly opened Reflecting Pool
sources.snippet: A day after refilling post-renovation, significant algae was visible; worker spotted clearing algae from the bottom.

sources.title: Nonprofit sues the federal government over plans to paint Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool blue
sources.snippet: TCLF sued NPS, DOI, and Secretary Burgum in U.S. District Court D.C. alleging failure to comply with the National Historic Preservation Act; seeks to halt the project.

sources.title: Beautification Out of the (American Flag) Blue: The Lawsuit That Attempted to Halt the Reflecting Pool Makeover
sources.snippet: Judge Carl J. Nichols did not issue TRO/injunction before completion June 3, 2026; May 21 hearing showed skepticism; case remains active but did not halt resurfacing.

sources.title: Trump ups Reflecting Pool renovation projections to 'less than $20 million' amid court fight
sources.snippet: Trump said cost will be 'less than $20 million'; comments came as federal judge heard arguments from nonprofit suing to stop the project.

sources.title: Lawsuit Filed to Halt Painting Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
sources.snippet: Washington Litigation Group filed emergency lawsuit May 11, 2026 on behalf of TCLF and CEO Charles A. Birnbaum to halt the blue repainting.

key_facts: As of July 14, 2026, the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is NOT fully repaired — it was drained a second time (starting July 10, 2026) for additional repairs, containing only algae and debris.
Trump announced the renovation in April 2026 with an initial estimate of ~$1-2 million and a 1-2 week timeline; the project was declared complete June 3, 2026 and refilled around June 5.
Within a day of refilling in June 2026, significant algae bloomed; DOI called it 'residual' algae from reactivated supply lines.
The 'American flag blue' polyurea coating began peeling within ~two weeks, with sections floating in the water; the liner appeared to be 'falling apart.'
Algae remediation used hydrogen peroxide and nanobubble ozone technology (a second contractor); the nanobubble treatment 'may have led to the new blue finish peeling off the bottom.'
Trump blamed the peeling on vandalism, alleging a '350-foot gash' in the liner, but when the pool was drained again 'no evidence of the gash had emerged.'
At least four people have been charged with defacing the monument, including former Olympic canoeist David Hearn, who pleaded not guilty.

The renovation contract of $14.7 million went to Atlantic Industrial Coatings of Virginia on a no-bid basis citing 'unusual and compelling urgency' for America's 250th anniversary; with algae cleanup (~$1.7M), total reached ~$16 million.
Trump revised the cost projection to 'less than $20 million' on May 21, 2026.
The Cultural Landscape Foundation (CEO Charles Birnbaum) sued NPS, DOI, and Secretary Doug Burgum on May 11, 2026, alleging violation of the National Historic Preservation Act.

Judge Carl J. Nichols did NOT issue a TRO/preliminary injunction in time; the project was completed June 3, 2026 before the court ruled. The case remains active/ongoing but did not halt the resurfacing.
For comparison, the 2012 pool restoration cost $34 million over two years and also experienced an algal bloom upon completion.

data_points.metric: Date pool drained for second round of repairs
Renovation contract cost (Atlantic Industrial Coatings, no-bid)
Total project cost including algae cleanup
Alleged vandalism gash length claimed by Trump
People charged with defacing the monument
